title: Puerto Banus No Speedboat Rental Licence Guide 2026
html:
For anyone planning a day on the water in Puerto Banus No, the speedboat rental licence question is the first hurdle. With prices starting around 250 EUR for a half-day, you can explore the Costa del Sol without a licence through a local operator. BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare verified options and book direct.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus No?

No licence is required for boats under 15hp in Spanish inland waters, but the real answer depends on the operator and boat. Spain requires a recreational licence (PER) for boats over 15hp unless you take a guided charter where the operator holds the exemption. For speedboat rentals, most local companies offer a “no licence” option: you are accompanied by a qualified skipper or the boat is restricted to a specific range. Always confirm with the provider before booking, as the rules can vary. You can boat rental Puerto Banus No for your chosen dates.

BoatHire24 lists only verified operators who clearly state their licence requirements, so you can filter by those that accept self-drive without a licence. This saves time and ensures your trip is legal.

Local tip: ask the operator if they provide a free skipper briefing for a few minutes, some include it in the price to meet Spanish regulations.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus No

Puerto Banus No is within easy reach of several well-served marinas and natural bays. The best departure points include Marina del Este, a quiet harbour east of Nerja; Puerto de la Duquesa, a modern marina near Manilva; and the historic port of Malaga. For a more secluded start, head to Playa de la Herradura in Almuñécar, known for its sheltered waters. Each offers different distances to coastal highlights such as Cabo de Gata or the cliffs of Maro.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat depends on your group size and how you plan to spend the day. Here are four recommended types available for licence-free rental in 2026.

  1. Rib tender, best for a quick coastal dash with two to three people, from 150 EUR half-day.
  2. Speedboat up to 300hp, ideal for thrill-seekers and small groups of up to five, from 250 EUR half-day.
  3. Motor cruiser 30ft+, perfect for a full-day family trip with eight guests, from 600 EUR full-day.
  4. Catamaran 40ft, spacious for parties of ten or more, from 900 EUR full-day with skipper.

If a fast open boat appeals, browse the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing on BoatHire24 for a popular model that combines agility with comfort. One option worth a look is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus No?

Prices range from 80 EUR for a shared tour to over 1,500 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht. The exact cost depends on the boat type, season, and duration. Typical price bands include:

  • Shared tour on a small motorboat: 80, 120 EUR per person for a two-hour trip.
  • Self-drive speedboat half-day (5 hours): 250, 400 EUR without licence.
  • Catamaran full-day charter (8 hours): 900, 1,500 EUR for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day: from 1,200 EUR for six passengers.

For a direct comparison of boats with no licence required, use a trusted platform such as boat rental Puerto Banus No to see real availability and prices.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Puerto Banus No?

The high season runs from June to September, when the Mediterranean wind known as the Poniente can pick up in the afternoon. Spring (April to May) and autumn (October) offer calmer seas and fewer crowds, with pleasant water temperatures. Many operators run from April to October, but some remain open year-round. Book well ahead for July and August to secure the best boats.

At a glance

  • Price range: 80 EUR (shared tour) to 1,500 EUR (crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: half-day (5h) or full-day (8h)
  • Capacity: up to 8 guests on a speedboat, 12+ on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus No

Can I rent a speedboat without a licence if I am a tourist?

Yes, many operators in Puerto Banus No offer licence-free rentals provided a skipper or escort is part of the booking.

Do I need to bring any documents to rent a boat?

You need a valid passport or ID, and sometimes a credit card deposit; the operator will confirm their exact requirements.

Is my own driving licence valid for renting a boat in Spain?

No, a car driving licence does not qualify for boat rental; a Spanish or international recreational boating licence is required for unsupervised use.

What happens if the weather is bad on my rental day?

Most operators allow free rescheduling or a full refund if the port authority closes the harbour due to wind or waves.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus No and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
